name: Thomas Vane
voice_prompt: Male, early forties, soft baritone. Educated, precise, unsettlingly calm. Speaks quietly, forcing listener to lean in. Long pauses before responses - not hesitation, control. Uses names with uncomfortable intimacy. No emotional volatility; even threats delivered softly. Voice carries weight without volume. Something wrong beneath the polish.
character_context: Antagonist. A stalker who believes himself a romantic. Charming surface, void beneath. His composure is predatory; his softness is threat. Never raises voice. The quieter he speaks, the more dangerous.
notes: Antagonist voice. Key contrast: speaks softer than anyone else but dominates every conversation. Pauses are power plays. Names pronounced slowly, like tasting them. When pleased: slight smile audible. Never angry - disappointment is his register for displeasure.
